title: Index `set` acts on `rand` function
description:
### Company or project name
N/A
### Describe what's wrong
```sql
CREATE TABLE set_idx_rand
(
s String,
INDEX s s TYPE set(0) GRANULARITY 1
)
ENGINE=MergeTree
ORDER BY tuple();
INSERT INTO set_idx_rand SELECT * FROM generateRandom('s String') LIMIT 10;
explain indexes=1 WITH rand()%1000==1 AS sampled SELECT * FROM set_idx_rand WHERE sampled SETTINGS use_skip_indexes=1;
explain indexes=1 WITH rand()%1000==1 AS sampled SELECT * FROM set_idx_rand WHERE sampled SETTINGS use_skip_indexes=0;
```
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/da159abf-83a6-4ee9-a85b-dfa97b4c1793
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Repro ^
### Expected behavior
Index `set` should not be used when condition atom contains `rand` function
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE set_idx_rand_v2 (id INTEGER, s TEXT, d DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO set_idx_rand_v2 SELECT number, CAST(number % 10 AS TEXT) FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS number FROM (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10) LIMIT 100);
SELECT * FROM set_idx_rand_v2 WHERE d IS NULL OR d > DATETIME('now', '-1 day');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM set_idx_rand_v2 WHERE s IN (SELECT s FROM set_idx_rand_v2 WHERE id < 50) AND RANDOM() % 1000 = 1;
